data.body:
PASS 1 review — G6 (Approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection): CONCUR with the prior G6 audit (timestamp 1782504839676544001). Re-grounded its three findings independently this pass and they hold:

(1) MECHANISM GAP confirmed — `FakeAcliClient` exposes ONLY read/fetch surfaces (`search_issues`, `get_comment_map`, `get_issuelinks_map`, `get_parent_map` at tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/jira_contract/_fakes.py:48-63) and its docstring scopes it to "driving fetcher.fetch_snapshot / compute_snapshot" through the inbound enrichment merge (_fakes.py:11-17). It has no outbound/apply surface, so "story A's fixtures/fake through the production path" cannot by itself produce the local→Jira→local half a BIDIRECTIONAL round-trip requires. The plan must specify how the outbound half is obtained.

(2) EXISTING-HARNESS reconciliation confirmed — an established hermetic round-trip seam chaining the real pure differs already exists (referenced by tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_link_sync.py:11; link sync wired in both directions per that module's docstring, lines 13-28). The plan never mentions it; an implementer risks duplicating or replacing working coverage.

(3) MISSING POSITIVE RATIONALE confirmed — the plan asserts the fake-through-fetch seam but states no rationale for why it is the right vehicle for a round-trip given the existing differ-chaining seam, making alternative-selection non-auditable (G6 requires a positive rationale, not a rejected-alternatives section).

Affirmations: the de-encode half is fit-for-purpose and grounded — the NOT-SYNCED/"as expected" guard is real and load-bearing at scripts/jira-pressure-test/e2e_field_validation_probe.sh:1543-1547 (doc mention at 1914); converting it to xfail/fail-when-the-gap-closes directly fixes the named defect. AC4 (round-trip outcomes, not whole-blob equality) is the soundly-stated anti-change-detector property. No duplicate findings re-filed.
